_note: Effective $/token rates for workhorse models, derived from a live calibration probe (scripts/baseline-rig/calibrate.mjs). Gateway /key/info.spend is async/batched (settles in ~2-7s), so these rates give a fast per-call ESTIMATE; the campaign reconciles the grand total against the settled cumulative spend and rescales the per-model split to sum exactly. Reasoning tokens are billed inside completion_tokens, so perTotalToken already includes the reasoning tax.

test/kimi-k2.5._calibration: Settled-measured 2026-07-18 (metered, unlike K3). 3 probes: analysis-02 delta $0.010517/3744tok=$2.809/M, docqa-hard-05 $0.021425/7572tok=$2.829/M, data-hard-01 $0.023929/8393tok=$2.851/M. Blended $2.835/M over 19709 tokens. Cheaper than K2.6 ($3.847/M), ~3.4x DeepSeek. Latency high on hard fixtures (180-200s). Requires the sk-6WzeJk... key.

test/kimi-k3._calibration: NOT settled-measured: the Ikey TEST gateway currently meters kimi-k3 at $0 (2 probes = ~6000 output tokens moved cumulative spend by exactly $0.000000 on 2026-07-18). This rate is the PUBLISHED output price ($15/M) applied as a conservative blended upper bound (output+reasoning dominate ~90% of tokens; input assumed cheaper, so real blend ~13-14/M). Used so the cost gate reflects REAL K3 economics, not the free test tier. Refine if the gateway starts metering or the input price is confirmed.

sample.note: probes analysis-02 (3943 tok), docqa-hard-05 (3106 tok), data-hard-01 (8756 tok, 7024 reasoning, 236s latency); settledDelta 0 on all — unmetered.

cliTax.note: fresh claude -p (effort low) overhead; CLI usage.input/output EXCLUDE cache tokens, so recorded B tokens are lean; B1 cost = total_cost_usd (incl tax), B2 cost = apiRates on recorded tokens
